heroHeadline: Somalia: April 14 Parliamentary Deadline 5 Days Away — Col. Nur Farey Assassinated, AFRICOM Hits 49 Q1 Strikes, HRP Only 13.4% Funded
heroSubtitle: Somalia's constitutional crisis enters its most critical window as the April 14 parliamentary mandate expiry approaches with no FGS-opposition agreement in sight. Al-Shabaab scored a significant blow April 5 when SNA 14th October Brigade commander Col. Nur Farey was assassinated by a suspected infiltrator near Afgooye. AFRICOM confirmed 49 airstrikes in Q1 2026 — a record-setting pace. The humanitarian situation deepened: OCHA's April 6 Situation Report No. 5 shows the $852M HRP only 13.4% funded; 4.4 million face IPC Phase 3+ food insecurity and the Gu rains offer only partial hope. Somalia faces concurrent constitutional, security, and humanitarian crises.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
